digest: Ensemble is a TypeScript library-first platform for managing Claude Code extensions across AI clients. It provides a central canonical store at `~/.config/ensemble/library/` for seven declarative resource types: MCP servers, skills, plugins, subagents, slash commands, hooks, and managed settings. Install state is modeled as a per-client/per-project property of each library resource, distinct from library membership. Resources carry dual-field annotations: source-owned `description` and user-authored `userNotes`. The project exposes pure-function operations via Zod schemas, a CLI with lifecycle verbs, and an Electron desktop app with a pivot-based sidebar and project × Claude Code matrix view. The desktop app uses a tRPC bridge for IPC, React, and Tailwind CSS. Sync is additive-only with non-destructive settings.json merge, safe apply, and rollback snapshots. Drift detection uses artifact-level content hashes. Registry integration supports the Official MCP Registry, Glama, and claude-plugins.dev for skills. Optional read-only integrations exist for setlist capability awareness and the project registry. The v2.0.1 target modules (agents, commands, hooks, settings, snapshots, doctor, browse) are built. The v1.3 install-state verbs (enableServer, disableServer, etc.) remain in exports pending a coordinated rename sweep. The `import-legacy.ts` module is a throwaway transitional subsystem.
